narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Washington “cannot afford” to allow Russia to achieve victory in the Ukraine conflict as this would mean losing direct access to vast mineral assets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text frames the US motivation for supporting Ukraine as a resource grab, implying the West is fighting for minerals rather than Ukraine defending itself, thereby shifting blame for the conflict's continuation away from Russia's invasion and onto Western economic interests.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: "Well, they will feed the corrupt top of the neo-Nazi regime in Kiev at the same time."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote directly attacks the legitimacy and character of the Ukrainian government by labeling it as both 'corrupt' and a 'neo-Nazi regime', which aligns with the narrative of discrediting Ukraine.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Graham is treating Ukraine as a future colony of the US with his comments on the ownership of the country's natural resources... "The fate of the colonies is always the same: the Americans will scoop up all the natural resources of Ukraine in exchange for 'the great honor of being a friend of the United States'"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text, through the Russian official's statement, criticizes and portrays US foreign policy and intentions as colonialist and exploitative, directly discrediting Western motives and diplomacy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Washington “cannot afford” to allow Russia to achieve victory in the Ukraine conflict as this would mean losing direct access to vast mineral assets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote, presented as the core argument of the article, shifts the cause of the war from Russia's invasion to Western (specifically US) economic greed and desire to control resources, thereby blaming the West for the continuation of the conflict.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: "Graham is treating Ukraine as a future colony of the US with his comments on the ownership of the country's natural resources, said Vladimir Dzhabarov, official of the Russian Federation Council."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The article directly quotes a Russian official accusing a US Senator of colonialist intentions, framing Western aid as predatory and exploitative rather than supportive, which discredits Western motives and diplomacy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: "Well, they will feed the corrupt top of the neo-Nazi regime in Kiev at the same time."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly attacks the legitimacy of the Ukrainian government by labeling it as both 'corrupt' and a 'neo-Nazi regime', which is a common narrative used to undermine Ukraine's sovereignty and justify aggression against it.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Graham is treating Ukraine as a future colony of the US with his comments on the ownership of the country's natural resources, said Vladimir Dzhabarov, official of the Russian Federation Council.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes and discredits Western (specifically US) diplomatic and military support for Ukraine by framing it as a colonialist, exploitative endeavor rather than genuine assistance.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: "Well, they will feed the corrupt top of the neo-Nazi regime in Kiev at the same time."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement attacks the legitimacy of the Ukrainian government by labeling it as both 'corrupt' and a 'neo-Nazi regime,' which aligns with the narrative of undermining Ukraine's actions and intentions as a nation.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Washington “cannot afford” to allow Russia to achieve victory in the Ukraine conflict as this would mean losing direct access to vast mineral assets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This framing, presented as the view of a US Senator and amplified by the article's title, shifts the primary cause of the conflict from Russia's invasion to Western (specifically US) economic interests and greed for resources.
